task_completion.description: Navigate to the Basic Page creation form
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Drupal admin interface showing the 'Create Basic page' or 'Add content' form.

task_completion.description: Create a Basic Page titled 'Terms and Conditions' with placeholder text
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message stating 'Basic page Terms and Conditions has been created' or the published page itself showing the title 'Terms and Conditions' and some placeholder legal text.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Checkout Flow configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Drupal Commerce Checkout flows configuration screen (e.g., editing the 'Default' checkout flow at Commerce > Configuration > Orders > Checkout flows).

task_completion.description: Enable the 'Terms of service' checkout pane
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Terms of service' pane is visible in an active checkout step (e.g., 'Review' or 'Order information') and is no longer in the 'Disabled' section of the checkout flow builder.

task_completion.description: Link the 'Terms of service' pane to the created page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration modal or settings section for the 'Terms of service' pane showing the 'Terms and Conditions' page selected or referenced as the linked terms page.

integrity.description: Agent used the Drupal web interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser (e.g., clicking through Drupal admin menus) rather than using terminal commands like Drush to create nodes or modify configurations.

integrity.description: Page creation occurred on the actual Drupal site
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'http://localhost' and the standard Drupal admin theme (e.g., Claro or Seven) is visible during content creation.

integrity.description: Agent configured the checkout flow via the UI, not by editing files
integrity.visual_evidence: The checkout flow changes are made using the drag-and-drop UI in the browser, not by editing YAML configuration files or database tables directly in a terminal or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected page title is exactly 'Terms and Conditions'. Note: The specific pane ID 'commerce_checkout_terms_of_service' could not be verified as a standard Drupal Commerce pane in the backend, so you must rely entirely on visual evidence of a 'Terms of service' pane being enabled and linked in the Drupal Commerce Checkout Flow UI.
